content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
December 16, 1985 
Tom M. Esqueda, Treasurer 
Richard G. Pierce for Sheriff committee 
1806 North Vagedes 
Fresno, 
CA 
93705 
Re: 
FPPC No. A-85-240 
Dear Mr. Esqueda: 
Thank you for your letter of November 20, 1985, asking if 
there are any prohibitions contained in the Political Reform Act 
which would prevent a campaign committee from paying campaign 
committee consultants a salary based on a percentage of the total 
amount of money they individually raise for a candidate. 
As I mentioned in our telephone conversation, the Political 
Reform Act does not prohibit such expenditures. 
The committee 
must report the total of all contributions it.receives. In the 
case of those contributions raised by consultants, the total 
amount of the contribution (before deducting consultants' 
commissions) must be reported, itemizing any donor that 
contributes $100 or more within a calendar year. 
Likewise, all 
expenditures must be reported, itemizing expenditures of $100 or 
more. 
To determine if $100 or more has been expended to anyone 
consultant, a series of payments made to a consultant during the 
reporting period must be cumulated. 
The consultants, if acting as an agent or intermediary for 
the contributions they receive, must provide the committee's 
treasurer with the names, addresses and occupations of donors 
contributing $25.00 or more. 
The consultants must also report 
promptly the contributions they receive on behalf of the candidate 
to the committee's treasurer. 
Promptly is defined' as no later 
than the closing date of the next campaign statement required to 
be filed by the candidate and his or her controlled committee. 
I hope this answers your questions and that you will not 
hesi tate to contact me if you have additional que.stions. 
Sincer~l'

Sacramento, California 95814 
To Whom It May Concern: 
Attached is a copy of a letter sent to you requesting clarification 
of fees that can be paid to campaign employees for fund raising.

Treasurer

Enclosures 


November 20. 1985 
Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street 
Sacramento. California 95814 
To Whom It Hay Concern: 
Are there provisions governing the mode a campaign utilizes to determine 
level of fees paid to campaign employees who are consultants by definition? 
Specifically, we wish to retain part-time consultants who fund raise for 
a sheriff's campaign, and them at a level of 5% of the net funds they 
individually raise. 
We were informed by Counselor Hary Ann that she saw no problem with it 
as long as such fees were reported as expenditures. Please respond 
in writing. 
Sincerely. 
Treasurer 
Richard G. Pierce for 
Sheriff Committee 
11850745
